observation.messy_prompt:
Customer email: Need 10,000 units by day 12. Same ABS as last time. Can you do $0.20/unit?
Operator note: M4 sounded rough this afternoon; might need maintenance.
Inventory: ABS=220kg, Nylon=80kg, PP=40kg.
Machine log: M1 uptime=95%, M2 uptime=90%, M3 uptime=92%, M4 uptime=88%.
HR: operator O1 out sick one day this period.
Current jobs: J100 due day 2 med priority; J133 due day 2 med priority; J102 due day 3 low priority.

job_source.license: CC BY 4.0; full dataset requires armbench.com registration
job_source.note: real Amazon warehouse singulation picks: container manifest + GT product ID mapped to tote→arm→tray pick jobs

mapping_profile.name: MAPF grid warehouse / ARMBench pick face
mapping_profile.mapping_quality: plan-specific heuristic: order streams are constrained to fixture zones from this floor-plan backdrop

job_source.summary: Imported 230 ARMBENCH order lines across 200 orders (SKU references and quantities drawn from real ARMBENCH-SCHEMA orders), mapped to mapf-warehouse-20-40-10-2-1 (MAPF grid warehouse / ARMBench pick face): 62 source zones, 5 stations, 3 route constraints, 3 job families, layout pressure 0.743.

provenance.license: public research dataset; verify upstream license before redistribution
provenance.note: warehouse scene-graph inspired fixture, adapted into ShiftBench symbolic operations

intake.scenario_note: Imported 230 ARMBENCH order lines across 200 orders (SKU references and quantities drawn from real ARMBENCH-SCHEMA orders), mapped to mapf-warehouse-20-40-10-2-1 (MAPF grid warehouse / ARMBench pick face): 62 source zones, 5 stations, 3 route constraints, 3 job families, layout pressure 0.743.
intake.scenario: Large MAPF fulfillment grid: high-volume ARMBench picks compete for dock staging and charger loops on a 321×123 tile map.
